maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves Action1 objects, reports, and templates.
`Get-Action1` retrieves Action1 objects and data from the API, as well as helper templates used for creating or modifying resources.
Depending on the `-Query` value, the command can:
- Retrieve Action1 objects such as endpoints, policies, reports, packages, and automations.
- Generate configuration templates used with other module commands such as `New-Action1` or `Update-Action1`.

When `-Query Settings` is used, the function returns template objects for different Action1 resource types (for example, EndpointGroup, Automation, Remediation, DeploySoftware). These templates may include helper script methods that simplify modifying the object before sending it back to the API.
Pagination is handled automatically for long list queries.
Some returned Action1 objects include helper methods for convenience:
The default Action1 organization is expected to be set in advance by `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`
Specifies the type of Action1 object or dataset to retrieve.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 object to retrieve.
Used for queries that return a specific Action1 resource instance.
Specifies the maximum number of records requested per API call.
This parameter maps to the API limit query argument and can be used to control page size when retrieving large datasets.
Specifies the Action1 resource type when retrieving Action1 template objects using `-Query Settings`.
Specifies the ID of an existing Action1 object to clone when generating templates.
This parameter is only valid when `-Query Settings` is used.
Specifies the type of Action1 object or dataset to retrieve.
Specifies the identifier of the Action1 object to retrieve.
Used for queries that return a specific Action1 resource instance.
Specifies the Action1 resource type when retrieving Action1 template objects using `-Query Settings`.
Specifies the ID of an existing Action1 object to clone when generating templates.
This parameter is only valid when `-Query Settings` is used.
Specifies the maximum number of records requested per API call.
This parameter maps to the API limit query argument and can be used to control page size when retrieving large datasets.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
System.Object. Returns objects retrieved from the API.

maml:description.maml:para: The returned type depends on the value of `-Query` parameter. Some objects may include helper script methods for additional operations.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires valid API credentials set with `Set-Action1Credentials`
The default Action1 organization is expected to be set in advance via `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`
Pagination is automatically processed for long list queries.
Some queries return raw API responses while others return processed objects with helper methods.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns all endpoints belonging to the current Action1 organization.
Retrieves a specific Action1 endpoint by ID.
Executes a direct Action1 API GET call using the specified URI.
Returns a new Action1 Endpoint Group template object that can be customized and used with `New-Action1` command.
Creates a new Action1 Endpoint Group template cloned from an existing group with Id "group-id"
Retrieves Action1 policy results and then fetches the detailed records for each result.
Retrieves Action1 endpoints with an API page size of 50 objects.

maml:description.maml:para: `New-Action1` creates a new object in the remote API using a POST request.
The type of object being created is specified by the Item parameter.
Depending on the selected item type, the function automatically determines the correct API endpoint using the module's internal URI lookup table.
If `Item` is set to RawURI , the request is sent directly to the URI specified by the URI parameter, bypassing the internal lookup logic.
The command requires valid authentication credentials and a valid access token. Use `Set-Action1Credentials` and any required authentication commands before calling this function.
Specifies the type of object to create.
Specifies the API endpoint path when Item is set to `RawURI`.
This parameter is ignored for other item types because the function determines the URI automatically.
Specifies the request payload that will be sent to the API.
The structure depends on the type of item being created.
Specifies the type of object to create.
Specifies the API endpoint path when Item is set to `RawURI`.
This parameter is ignored for other item types because the function determines the URI automatically.
Specifies the request payload that will be sent to the API.
The structure depends on the type of item being created.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
System.Object. Returns the API response object returned by the PushData request.

maml:alert.maml:para: This function:
Uses the module's internal URI lookup table to determine API endpoints.
Requires a valid Action1 authentication token previously specified by `Set-Action1Credentials`.
Sends data using an HTTP POST request.
If the `RawURI` item type is used, the valid `URI` value must be supplied.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new Action1 organization using the data provided in $OrgData
Creates a new Action1 endpoint group.
Sends a POST request directly to a custom Action1 API endpoint using the provided payload.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets Action1 API credentials for the current PowerShell session.
Stores Action1 APIKey and Secret in script scope variables used by other commands in the module to authenticate API calls.
Action1 API key provided by the service.
Action1 API secret associated with the Action1 API key.
Action1 API key provided by the service.
Action1 API secret associated with the Action1 API key.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command only sets module and session variables.

maml:alert.maml:para: Action1 credentials are stored only for the lifetime of the PowerShell session.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stores Action1 credentials for use by other PSAction1 module commands.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables debug mode for PSAction1 module.
Sets the internal debug flag for the PSAction1 module in script scope.
When debug mode is enabled, additional diagnostic messages will be displayed by module functions that support debugging output.
Specifies whether the debug mode should be enabled or disabled.
Specifies whether the debug mode should be enabled or disabled.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command does not return objects. It only updates an internal module variable controlling debug behavior.

maml:alert.maml:para: The debug mode affects only the current PowerShell session and is stored in a script-scope variable.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the debug mode for PSAction1 module.
Disables the debug mode for PSAction1 module.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the default Action1 organization ID for the current PowerShell session.

Stores the specified Action1 organization ID in a script scope variable used by other commands in the module. Once set, the value can be reused by commands that require an organization identifier, so it does not need to be specified repeatedly.
The value is stored only for the duration of the current PowerShell session.
The identifier of the Action1 organization to use as the default for subsequent module commands.
The identifier of the Action1 organization to use as the default for subsequent module commands.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command does not produce output. It only sets a module-level variable.

maml:alert.maml:para: The default organization value is stored in a script scope variable and is
available only for the lifetime of the PowerShell session.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the default organization ID to org-12345 for use by other module commands.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables interactive mode for PSAction1 module.
Enables or disables interactive mode for the current PowerShell session.

When interactive mode is enabled, the PSAction1 module will prompt for the required variable values that have not been set. This helps guide users through configuration when running commands that depend on module-level variables.
When interactive mode is disabled, commands will not prompt for missing values and may fail if required variables are not set.
Action1 interactive setting is stored in a script-scoped variable and applies only to the current PowerShell session.
Specifies whether PSAction1 interactive mode should be enabled or disabled.
Specifies whether PSAction1 interactive mode should be enabled or disabled.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command only modifies a module-level setting.

maml:alert.maml:para: The interactive mode is stored in a script-scoped variable and remains active only for the lifetime of the PowerShell session.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables interactive mode. The module will prompt for required variables that are missing.
Disables interactive mode so commands will not prompt for missing variables.

maml:description.maml:para: (DEPRECATED!) Sets the Action1 locale used by the PSAction1 module for the current PowerShell session. Please use Set-Action1Region instead.
This function is deprecated. `Set-Locale` previously configured the locale(region) used by the PSAction1 module for API requests and regional formatting.
This command is retained for backward compatibility but will be removed in a
future version of the PSAction1 module.
Specifies the locale/region identifier to use.
Specifies the locale/region identifier to use.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None: This command only sets a module variable for the current session.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
Use Set-Action1Region instead.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the module locale, actually region, to "NorthAmerica" for the current session.
Sets the module locale, actually region, to "Europe" for the current session.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the Action1 API region endpoint used by the PSAction1 module.
`Set-Action1Region` configures the base API endpoint used by the module for all subsequent requests.
Different Action1 regions correspond to different service API endpoints.
This command updates the internal script scope variable so that all module commands send requests to the correct regional API endpoint.
Run this command before executing other commands in the module to ensure a specific regional environment is targeted.
Specifies the API region to use.
Specifies the API region to use.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command only sets an internal module variable used to determine the API base URI.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command must typically be executed before making API requests so that PSAction1 module communicates with the correct regional service endpoint.
The selected region affects all subsequent commands in the current PowerShell session.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Configures PSAction1 module to use the Europe API endpoint.
Configures PSAction1 module to use the NorthAmerica API endpoint.
Configures PSAction1 module to use the Australia API endpoint.

maml:description.maml:para: Uploads an Action1 package file to the software repository in chunks.
`Start-Action1PackageUpload` uploads an Action1 package file to the remote software repository for a specific package version.
The upload `Platform` must be specified as `Windows_32` or `Windows_64`.

The upload is performed using a resumable chunked upload process . Chunked uploads allow large files to be transferred reliably and provide progress feedback during the upload process. The chunk size can be controlled using the `BufferSize` parameter (default is 24MB).
This command requires a valid Action1 API authentication token (set earlier in the session). See `Set-Action1Credentials`.
Identifier of the package in the software repository.
Identifier of the version of the package that the file will be uploaded to.
Path to the file that will be uploaded.
Target platform for the package upload.
Size of the upload chunk buffer in bytes.
Larger buffers reduce the number of HTTP requests but increase memory usage.
Identifier of the package in the software repository.
Identifier of the version of the package that the file will be uploaded to.
Path to the file that will be uploaded.
Target platform for the package upload.
Size of the upload chunk buffer in bytes.
Larger buffers reduce the number of HTTP requests but increase memory usage.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command performs the upload operation and reports progress using debug output.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires a valid authentication token in the current session.
The upload is performed using a resumable chunked transfer mechanism.
Progress information is displayed through Debug-Host messages.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Uploads the file App.msi as version 1.0.0 of package 12345 for the Windows 64-bit platform.
Uploads the installer using a larger 48 MB upload buffer to reduce the number of upload chunks.

maml:description.maml:para: Triggers a requery operation to refresh specific data from Action1 endpoints or the organization.
`Start-Action1Requery` triggers a requery request to the API to refresh specific datasets.
Depending on the selected Type , the command may operate either organization-wide or for a specific endpoint.
The default organization must be set in advance via `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`.
This command sends a POST request to the appropriate API endpoint and instructs the service to regenerate or refresh the requested data.
- InstalledSoftware - Refreshes installed software inventory for endpoints.
If the selected requery type supports endpoint targeting, an Endpoint_Id may be supplied.
If not supplied, the requery will run at the organization level.
Authentication must be configured before using this command.
Specifies the type of requery operation to perform.
Specifies the endpoint identifier for endpoint-specific data refresh.
If omitted, the operation is executed at the organization level.
If the selected requery type does not support endpoint targeting, the parameter is ignored and the request defaults to a default Action1 organization-wide operation.
Specifies the type of requery operation to perform.
Specifies the endpoint identifier for endpoint-specific data refresh.
If omitted, the operation is executed at the organization level.
If the selected requery type does not support endpoint targeting, the parameter is ignored and the request defaults to a default Action1 organization-wide operation.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
System.Object. Returns the API response from the requery request.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command requires a valid authentication token to be set prior to execution.
Use the credentials configuration command before invoking API actions.
The default organization might be set in advance via `Set-Action1DefaultOrg`.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Triggers default Action1 organization-wide refresh of the report data.
Triggers a refresh of installed software information for the specified Action1 endpoint with Id "12345"

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> PS C:\> Start-Action1Requery -Type InstalledUpdates

dev:remarks.maml:para: Triggers a refresh of installed update information for all applicable endpoints in the Action1 default organization.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates, modifies membership, or deletes Action1 objects.
`Update-Action1` performs update operations against Action1 objects exposed through the API.
Depending on the `-Action` and `-Type` parameters, the command can:
- Send a raw PATCH request to a specified URI.

The function builds the appropriate API endpoint path internally and sends the request using the module's internal API helper functions. Authentication must be configured in advance using `Set-Action1Credentials`.
For destructive actions such as `Delete`, the command prompts for confirmation unless the `-Force` switch is specified.
Specifies the type of operation to perform.
Specifies the target object type for the operation.
Object containing data to send in the API request body.
For `Modify` and `ModifyMembers`, this usually contains the updated properties or membership list.
Identifier of the object being modified or deleted.
Name of the custom attribute when updating an endpoint custom attribute.
Value to assign to the specified custom attribute.
Raw API URI used when `-Type RawURI` is specified.
Suppresses the confirmation prompt when performing `Delete` action.
Specifies the type of operation to perform.
Specifies the target object type for the operation.
Object containing data to send in the API request body.
For `Modify` and `ModifyMembers`, this usually contains the updated properties or membership list.
Identifier of the object being modified or deleted.
Name of the custom attribute when updating an endpoint custom attribute.
Value to assign to the specified custom attribute.
Raw API URI used when `-Type RawURI` is specified.
Suppresses the confirmation prompt when performing `Delete` action.

dev:type.maml:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
System.Object Returns the response object from the API request.

maml:alert.maml:para: The command requires a valid authentication token. Use Set-Action1Credentials cmdlet before executing API operations.
Some operations (such as endpoint updates) automatically filter allowed fields before sending the request to the API.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the name of the Action1 endpoint with the ID "endpoint-123".
Updates properties of the Action1 endpoint group with the ID "group-42".
Adds or modifies members of the Action1 endpoint group with the ID "group-42".
Updates a custom attribute "Owner" for the specified Action1 endpoint with the ID "endpoint-123".
Prompts for confirmation and deletes the specified Action1 automation object with the ID "auto-88".
Deletes Action1 endpoint with the ID "endpoint-123" without prompting for confirmation.
Sends a PATCH request directly to the specified Action1 API URI "/v1/custom/path".
